title: CCPA/CPRA — Correction/Deletion Verification — Downstream Data Warehouse Row Still Present After Deletion Certificate

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → '§1798.105'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `index=privacy` `sourcetype="privacy:delete_job"` (subject_id, job_id, completed_ts, systems_touched); `index=dataops` `sourcetype="dq:row_probe"` (subject_id, dataset, found_flag, probe_ts)
app: Splunk DB Connect (read-only probes), deletion orchestrator HEC
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="privacy:delete_job" earliest=-90d
| eval done=strptime(completed_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left subject_id [
    search index=dataops sourcetype="dq:row_probe" earliest=-90d
    | eval pt=strptime(probe_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | where found_flag=true
    | fields subject_id, dataset, found_flag, pt
  ]
| where pt>done
| stats values(dataset) as datasets by subject_id, job_id
| sort subject_id
value: Deletion rights require proof that replicas disappear. Comparing deletion job completion events to late-arriving warehouse snapshots (row presence tests) shows the firm validates end-state—not only API 200 responses from a primary system.
implementation: (1) Use salted `subject_id` only; (2) probes must be authorized read-only queries; (3) redact outputs; (4) auto-reopen deletion jobs; (5) document in ROPA technical measures.
visualization: Table (failed verifications), Single value (subjects), Bar by dataset.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
